sector_outlook: Consumer staples are experiencing a defensive rotation in 2026, with XLP up roughly 14% YTD through early April as investors seek safety amid recession fears and tariff uncertainty. However, the sector forward P/E of 22.3x (per MacroMicro, March 2026) is more nuanced than the headline 27.5x trailing figure suggests -- sub-sectors like Household Products trade below their 3-year average of 25.2x while retailers like COST (53x) and WMT (46x) are severely stretched. The critical dynamic is the transition from pricing-led to volume-led growth: companies that can deliver real organic volume growth (PM, COST, KO) will outperform, while those relying on residual pricing power face margin compression as private label captures 24% unit share (up from 21% in 2024). Input cost relief is materializing with cocoa projected down 6% and arabica coffee down 13% in 2026, which should benefit margins in H2. GLP-1 drug adoption (23% of US households now have a user) is a structural headwind for snacking and sugary beverages, with household grocery spending falling 5.3% within 6 months of starting treatment. The biggest risk is valuation compression if recession fears dissipate and capital rotates back to growth/tech -- a reversion to 20x sector PE would imply 10-28% downside for premium-valued names.
key_themes: Defensive rotation driving premium multiples: Capital flowing from tech into staples, but creating a valuation trap for buyers at 40-53x PE in retailers
Volume recovery is the new battleground: After 2 years of aggressive pricing, consumer elasticity limits further hikes; real unit growth separates winners from losers
Smoke-free transformation creating a growth anomaly: PM's IQOS/ZYN delivering 11-13% EPS growth, an outlier in a 3-5% growth sector
GLP-1 structural disruption: 23% household penetration reducing snack/soda consumption 5-10%, creating permanent category headwinds for PEP Frito-Lay and KO sparkling
Private label intensification: Store brands at $283B (24% unit share) growing 3x faster than national brands, pressuring pricing power across the sector
Input cost normalization: Cocoa -6%, coffee -13% projected in 2026, providing margin tailwind after 2 years of cost pressure
risks: Valuation compression risk: If sector PE reverts from 22.3x forward to 18-20x (historical trough), broad-based 10-20% drawdowns are likely
Tariff escalation: Trade tensions could increase input costs for globally sourced commodities, packaging materials, and cross-border supply chains
GLP-1 adoption acceleration: Broader insurance coverage and oral formulations could push household penetration past 30% by 2028, structurally reducing food/beverage volumes
Private label permanent share shift: Once consumers switch to store brands, national brands face uphill battle to win back share -- 24% and climbing
Interest rate sensitivity: If 10Y yields remain above 4.5%, staples' 2-4% dividend yields lose relative attractiveness versus risk-free alternatives

valuation_heat_map.description: Current PE vs 5-year average vs 10-year average per pick. Red = significantly above historical average, Yellow = near average, Green = below average

COST.heat: RED - Significantly above all historical averages

PM.heat: YELLOW-RED - Premium justified by smoke-free transformation but stretched vs history

KO.heat: GREEN - In line with historical averages

WMT.heat: DEEP RED - More than double 10yr average PE

CL.heat: YELLOW-RED - Above historical range with only 1.4% revenue growth

PEP.heat: GREEN - Trading well below 5yr and 10yr averages

MDLZ.heat: GREEN - Below historical averages on cocoa headwinds

HSY.heat: DEEP RED - Earnings collapsed on cocoa costs, PE artificially inflated

GIS.heat: DEEP GREEN - Severely discounted vs all historical averages

glp1_impact_analysis.methodology: Based on Circana household panel data, GLP-1 users reduce grocery spending by 5.3% average within 6 months. Category-level impacts applied to each pick's revenue exposure.

PEP.mitigation: Pivoting to permissible snacking (SunChips, PopCorners, Siete), but legacy Lay's/Doritos remain core

KO.mitigation: Zero-sugar variants now 30%+ of sparkling volume; functional beverages (BodyArmor) offset

MDLZ.mitigation: Portion control packs, premium dark chocolate positioning, 40% EM exposure dilutes US GLP-1 impact

COST.mitigation: Membership model insulates from category-level volume shifts; fresh/organic growing

WMT.mitigation: Private label flexibility allows rapid category mix shift; pharmacy dispensing GLP-1s adds revenue

PM.exposed_segment: N/A - tobacco/nicotine not impacted by GLP-1

CL.exposed_segment: N/A - oral/personal care not impacted by GLP-1

HSY.mitigation: Limited -- core business is indulgent treats with no credible health pivot

GIS.mitigation: Pet food (Blue Buffalo) and meal solutions offset; portfolio less snack-heavy than PEP

fee_elasticity.historical_churn_on_increase: Less than 1% churn on 2014 increase; early 2024/2025 data shows minimal impact
fee_elasticity.price_elasticity_assessment: Highly inelastic -- 92%+ renewal through multiple price increases
fee_elasticity.online_signup_churn_risk: Management noted online signups have modestly higher churn, could create 50-100bps renewal headwind into FY2027

competitive_threats.amazon_prime: Overlapping membership model but different value proposition (delivery vs warehouse)
competitive_threats.walmart_plus: Growing competitor but lacks the treasure-hunt experience and bulk value
competitive_threats.sams_club: Direct competitor, being reinvested by WMT; potential share shift in suburban markets

pm_iqos_fda_analysis.current_status: IQOS Iluma PMTA filed October 2023; pending FDA review for 2+ years

full_approval.rationale: Previous IQOS versions received PMTA and MRTPA; FDA modified exposure claim still supported by evidence; Iluma is technological evolution not revolution

conditional_approval_with_restrictions.rationale: FDA may impose marketing restrictions, flavor limitations, or age verification requirements

delayed_or_additional_data_required.rationale: New device technology may trigger additional testing requirements
delayed_or_additional_data_required.eps_impact: Neutral near-term; defers US revenue to 2027+

denial.rationale: Low probability given prior approvals, but political/regulatory climate could shift
denial.eps_impact: -$0.10 on sentiment; immaterial to financials given non-US IQOS is 100% of current revenue

beverages.threat_level: LOW - Strong brand loyalty in beverages

tobacco_nicotine.threat_level: VERY LOW - Regulatory barriers to entry

oral_care.threat_level: LOW - Brand trust critical in health categories

private_label_competition.switching_dynamics: Once consumers switch to private label, national brands face uphill battle to win back share; quality perception gap has narrowed significantly

cocoa.price_trend: Surged 300% 2024-2025; now correcting, down 31% in Feb 2026
cocoa.margin_impact: HSY gross margins compressed 800bps in 2025; normalization could restore 300-500bps in H2 2026

coffee_arabica.price_trend: Record highs in 2025; Brazil projecting record 66.2M bag harvest for 2026 (+17.1%)
coffee_arabica.margin_impact: JDE Peet's absorbed EUR 1.6B cost inflation in 2025; relief expected H2 2026

corn.price_trend: Relatively stable; tariff risk is primary concern
corn.margin_impact: Modest -- corn is a smaller input vs cocoa/coffee for this pick set

tobacco_leaf.margin_impact: Minimal -- PM's shift to smoke-free reduces leaf dependency; IQOS uses reconstituted tobacco

comparison.revenue_ratio: Amazon 10.6x Walmart (down from 15:1 four years ago)
comparison.penetration_gap: WMT at 1% of GMV vs AMZN at 8% -- massive runway for WMT
comparison.2026_incremental_capture: WMT + AMZN expected to capture 89% of incremental retail media spend
comparison.strategic_significance: Ad revenue is the highest-margin business for both; WMT's 240M weekly visits create unmatched 1P data asset

walmart_ad_revenue_analysis.implication_for_wmt_valuation: If WMT reaches 3% ad penetration (still well below AMZN), ad revenue triples to ~$19B, adding $8-10 in incremental EPS at 75% margins. This optionality partially justifies the premium PE but requires flawless execution over 3-5 years.

scenario_analysis.description: Impact analysis if sector PE compresses from current levels to 20x (historical mean) vs staying elevated at current levels

scenario_a_pe_compression_to_20x.trigger: Risk-on rotation, recession fears dissipate, rates stay higher for longer

COST.note: Premium retailer PE floor is 35x, not 20x

KO.note: Dividend King yield expansion to ~3.5% provides floor

WMT.note: Highest risk; PE floor ~28x given transformation story

CL.note: Growth too slow to justify premium

PEP.note: Already near trough; limited additional downside

MDLZ.note: Near trough; cocoa normalization provides upside offset

HSY.note: Earnings need to recover first; PE misleading on depressed earnings

GIS.note: Already below compression target; contrarian upside

scenario_b_elevated_multiples_persist.trigger: Recession materializes, rates cut aggressively, safety premium justified

WMT.driver: Growth priced in; returns limited to EPS growth

CL.driver: Slow growth caps upside even with multiple support

PEP.driver: Multiple expansion from trough + 4% yield + EPS recovery

MDLZ.driver: Cocoa normalization + multiple expansion from trough

scenario_c_stagflation.trigger: Tariffs drive inflation higher while growth slows
scenario_c_stagflation.impact: Mixed -- staples pricing power is an advantage but input costs surge. PM and COST best positioned. HSY and MDLZ most vulnerable to renewed cocoa spike. PEP and GIS benefit from lower commodity exposure.

position_sizing.methodology: Risk-parity weighted by volatility and conviction score
position_sizing.rebalance_frequency: Quarterly or on 20%+ move in any position

entry_criteria.valuation: Forward PE within 10% of 5-year average OR at/below 10-year average
entry_criteria.momentum: RSI between 30-50 (oversold to neutral) on weekly timeframe
entry_criteria.fundamentals: Organic revenue growth positive; free cash flow yield > 3%

exit_criteria.valuation_ceiling: Forward PE exceeds 10-year average by 30%+ with no growth acceleration
exit_criteria.fundamental_deterioration: Two consecutive quarters of negative organic revenue growth
exit_criteria.thesis_break: Key catalyst fails (e.g., PM IQOS denied, COST renewals drop below 90%)
exit_criteria.stop_loss: 15% drawdown from entry triggers review; 20% triggers mandatory exit

COST.rationale: Reduced size due to valuation risk

HSY.rationale: SELL -- cocoa crisis + GLP-1 double whammy

top_picks.name: Philip Morris International Inc.
top_picks.debt_to_equity_note: Negative equity due to aggressive buybacks and Swedish Match acquisition debt; total debt $50B vs negative shareholder equity of -$9B. Not a traditional solvency concern -- FCF of $13.5B covers obligations 3.7x
top_picks.roe_note: Negative ROE is an artifact of negative equity, not operational weakness. Adjusted ROIC on invested capital (excluding goodwill distortion) is approximately 25-30%, best-in-class for staples
top_picks.bull_case: Smoke-free transformation delivers outlier growth: IQOS HTU shipments up 11% to 155B units across 106 markets, ZYN US volumes up 37% to 11.9B pouches with 61.5% market share. Smoke-free products now 41.5% of $40.6B revenue, growing toward majority by 2028. 2026 guidance of 11-13% adjusted EPS growth ($8.38-$8.53) is 2-3x the sector average. $13.5B operating cash flow comfortably funds both the 3.56% dividend and debt reduction. At 22x earnings, PM offers genuine growth-at-a-reasonable-price in a sector starved for growth.
top_picks.bear_case: Capital structure is aggressive: $50B debt with negative equity creates interest rate sensitivity. FDA IQOS Iluma decision is binary risk (55% probability of full approval, see analysis). Legacy combustible volumes declining 2-3% annually. Tobacco ESG exclusions limit institutional buyer universe. Competition from BAT (glo) and JT in heated tobacco intensifying. Nicotine pouch regulation risk at federal and state level could constrain ZYN growth.
top_picks.catalyst: FDA IQOS Iluma approval (55% probability, H1-H2 2026); ZYN capacity expansion enabling full US demand capture; 2026 guidance beat on smoke-free acceleration
top_picks.risk: FDA denial or material delay on IQOS; state-level nicotine pouch taxes; debt refinancing costs if rates stay elevated
top_picks.thesis: Philip Morris is the only consumer staples company delivering double-digit earnings growth through genuine structural transformation. IQOS and ZYN are category-defining products with dominant share, and at 22x PE with a 3.56% yield, the stock offers the best risk-adjusted return in the sector. The negative equity metrics are artifacts of capital structure, not operational weakness.

top_picks.name: PepsiCo, Inc.
top_picks.bull_case: Best valuation in mega-cap staples at 20.4x (20% below 5yr average of 25.5x) with a 4% Dividend King yield. Frito-Lay's $2B+ permissible snacking pivot (SunChips, PopCorners, Siete, Simply) positions for the GLP-1 era. Natural ingredient relaunch for Lay's and Tostitos in 2026 could drive volume recovery. Away-from-home channel is an untapped $10B+ opportunity. At this PE, bad news is priced in -- any positive catalyst drives multiple expansion.
top_picks.bear_case: Revenue growth of 2.3% is anemic. Frito-Lay North America faces a double headwind from GLP-1 (estimated -1.8% revenue drag) and private label share gains. 5.2% EPS growth is below PM and COST. Debt-to-equity of 2.0x is above sector average. International beverage operations face persistent FX drag. North America snack market share losses are accelerating in some sub-categories.
top_picks.catalyst: Lay's/Tostitos natural ingredient relaunch driving volume inflection; away-from-home expansion; potential activist interest given deep discount to intrinsic value
top_picks.risk: GLP-1 adoption accelerating beyond 30% household penetration; continued Frito-Lay market share losses; FX headwinds from emerging market currencies
top_picks.thesis: PepsiCo is the deepest value play in mega-cap staples, trading 20% below its 5-year average PE with a 4% Dividend King yield. The permissible snacking pivot directly addresses the GLP-1 headwind, and the depressed valuation provides a meaningful margin of safety that most sector peers lack.

top_picks.name: The Coca-Cola Company
top_picks.bull_case: Widest moat in consumer staples: 200+ country distribution, $30B+ brand portfolio, unmatched route-to-market. 2026 guidance of 4-5% organic revenue and 7-8% EPS growth is credible with EM acceleration (India and Latin America in double-digit organic growth). 63 consecutive dividend increases. At 25.3x, valuation is in line with 5yr average -- fair price for the highest quality franchise in the sector. Zero-sugar variants now 30%+ of sparkling volume, partially mitigating GLP-1 risk.
top_picks.bear_case: GLP-1 estimated -1.2% annual revenue drag on sparkling (MODERATE severity). $18B potential IRS tax liability is binary risk. Consumer downtrading in developed markets pressuring premium SKUs. Organic growth decelerating from post-COVID pricing highs. At 25x, stock prices in steady-state execution with limited upside surprise.
top_picks.catalyst: EM volume acceleration in India and Africa; IRS tax dispute resolution; zero-sugar innovation driving sparkling volume growth
top_picks.risk: IRS tax ruling at full liability; GLP-1 adoption exceeding projections in key US/EU markets; currency headwinds from EM exposure
top_picks.thesis: Coca-Cola's unrivaled global distribution and brand portfolio justify a fair-value PE. With EM driving double-digit growth and zero-sugar mitigating GLP-1 risk, the stock offers the most reliable compounding in the sector. Not cheap, but the highest quality.

top_picks.name: Costco Wholesale Corporation
top_picks.rating_change: DOWNGRADED from BUY -- valuation score reduced from 4 to 3; overall score corrected from 7.7 to 6.8 to properly weight the 53x PE
top_picks.bull_case: Membership model is a compounding machine: 92.2% US/Canada renewal, 14% fee revenue growth, 40.4M executive members growing 9.5%. E-commerce up 22.6%. International expansion (Asia double-digit comps) provides long runway. Historical fee elasticity is exceptional (<1% churn on price increases). Special dividend potential in late 2026.
top_picks.bear_case: At 53x trailing PE (30% above 5yr average, 53% above 10yr average), the stock is priced for perfection. Any growth deceleration below 10% EPS growth would trigger severe multiple compression -- scenario analysis shows 34.5% downside to PE floor of 35x. Online signups have modestly higher churn per management. Membership fee increase fully lapped. Sam's Club reinvestment by WMT is a credible competitive threat. Thin 2.8% margins leave zero room for execution error.
top_picks.catalyst: Special dividend; Asia comp acceleration; next membership fee increase cycle (2029-2030)
top_picks.risk: Multiple compression is the primary risk -- at 53x, the stock must deliver >12% EPS growth just to generate market returns. PE compression to 35x implies 34% downside even with earnings growth.
top_picks.thesis: Costco is an exceptional business at an excessive valuation. The membership model, renewal rates, and international expansion are best-in-class, but at 53x PE the stock prices in 3+ years of perfect execution. Downgraded to HOLD -- wait for a pullback to 40x or below for attractive risk-reward.
top_picks.scoring_note: V1 had valuation_score 4 and overall 7.7 which was internally inconsistent. A 53x PE stock with 0.5% yield cannot score above 7 overall regardless of quality. Corrected to 6.8 with valuation_score 3.

top_picks.name: Mondelez International, Inc.
top_picks.bull_case: Global #1 in biscuits, strong #2 in chocolate. 40% EM revenue provides structural volume growth. PE of 20.2x is 10% below 5yr average -- rare discount for this quality franchise. Cocoa prices projected -6% in 2026, providing margin relief after 800bps compression in 2025. $3B+ FCF supports buybacks and 3.55% yield. Emerging market double-digit organic growth over 5 years.
top_picks.bear_case: GLP-1 impact is MODERATE-HIGH with 60% of revenue in exposed categories (biscuits, chocolate). Cocoa normalization is not guaranteed -- weather disruption could extend crisis. Near-term guidance of flat-to-modest organic growth is a step-down. Private label competition intensifying in biscuits. Declining unit sales trend is concerning.
top_picks.catalyst: Cocoa cost normalization restoring 300-500bps gross margin; EM acceleration; capital return via buybacks
top_picks.risk: Renewed cocoa spike; GLP-1 penetration exceeding 30% of households; sustained unit volume declines in developed markets
top_picks.thesis: Mondelez is a quality global franchise temporarily discounted by cocoa headwinds. With PE 10% below its 5yr average and cocoa projected to decline 6% in 2026, the risk-reward is asymmetrically positive for patient investors.

top_picks.name: General Mills, Inc.
top_picks.rating_note: NEW ADDITION -- Deep value contrarian pick
top_picks.bull_case: PE of 10.4x is 41% below its 5yr average (17.5x) and 42% below 10yr average (18x) -- the deepest discount in the entire sector. In PE compression scenario, GIS is the only pick that goes UP because it is already below trough. Blue Buffalo pet food growing mid-single digits provides a growth engine. 3.8% dividend yield is safe at 55% payout ratio. If PE merely mean-reverts to 15x, the stock returns 44% before earnings growth.
top_picks.bear_case: Revenue declining -1.5% reflects genuine fundamental weakness in cereal and baking categories. Pet food competition from private label is increasing. Portfolio lacks the premium/health positioning of peers. GLP-1 impact is LOW-MODERATE but adds to headwinds. Management execution has been inconsistent. Debt-to-equity of 1.5x is above average.
top_picks.catalyst: PE mean reversion from 10.4x toward 15x; Blue Buffalo acceleration; cost savings driving margin expansion; potential takeout target at this valuation
top_picks.risk: Continued revenue declines; category secular decline in cereal/baking; dividend cut if FCF deteriorates
top_picks.thesis: General Mills is a deep value play trading at a generational discount to historical multiples. The market is pricing in permanent decline, but Blue Buffalo provides a growth offset and the 3.8% yield is well-covered. Mean reversion alone delivers 30%+ upside.

sell_recommendations.name: Walmart Inc.
sell_recommendations.rating_change: DOWNGRADED from BUY -- valuation is unjustifiable relative to growth profile
sell_recommendations.rationale: Walmart at 45.7x trailing PE (103% above 10yr average of 22.5x) is pricing in a decade of flawless transformation execution. Revenue growth of 4.9% does not justify a PE 2x above historical norms. The ad revenue story is compelling (Walmart Connect at $6.4B, growing 46%) but even tripling ad revenue to $19B would only add $8-10 in EPS -- insufficient to justify the current multiple when the stock already trades at 36x forward. In PE compression scenario, WMT faces 38.7% downside to its PE floor of 28x. Dividend yield of 1.0% offers no cushion versus 4.5%+ Treasuries.
sell_recommendations.sell_price_target_range: $75-85 (implies 28-32x forward PE, still premium to historical)
sell_recommendations.what_would_change_our_mind: Revenue acceleration to 8%+ driven by ad revenue inflection; margin expansion to 5%+ net margin; PE pullback to 30x forward

sell_recommendations.name: Colgate-Palmolive Company
sell_recommendations.roe_note: Inflated ROE due to minimal equity from aggressive buybacks; not indicative of operational efficiency
sell_recommendations.rating_change: DOWNGRADED from BUY -- growth does not justify premium valuation
sell_recommendations.rationale: Colgate at 33.4x PE with 1.4% revenue growth offers no alpha over Treasuries yielding 4.5%+. The total return math: 2.1% dividend yield + 3-4% EPS growth = 5-6% total return -- barely above the risk-free rate with equity downside risk. PE is 13% above 5yr average and 24% above 10yr average despite growth deceleration. Organic growth guidance of 1-4% is uninspiring and the bottom end (1%) would be below inflation. In PE compression scenario, CL faces 28% downside. The oral care franchise is genuinely high-quality but the stock is priced as a growth company when it is clearly a low-growth yield vehicle.
sell_recommendations.sell_price_target_range: $80-88 (implies 22-24x forward PE, in line with 10yr average)
sell_recommendations.what_would_change_our_mind: Organic growth acceleration to 5%+ sustained; acquisition that adds a genuine growth vertical; PE pullback to 24x

sell_recommendations.name: The Hershey Company
sell_recommendations.rating_note: NEW ADDITION -- Worst risk-reward in the sector
sell_recommendations.rationale: Hershey faces a triple headwind: (1) Cocoa prices surged 300% and remain elevated despite recent correction, compressing gross margins by 800bps in 2025; (2) GLP-1 exposure is VERY HIGH at 85% of revenue in indulgent confectionery with no credible health pivot; (3) PE of 52.2x is artificially inflated by depressed earnings, but even the forward PE of 28x is 12% above the 10yr average of 25x. Revenue is declining -2% with unit volumes falling. The company has virtually no mitigation for GLP-1 -- its entire business is indulgent treats that are exactly the category GLP-1 users cut first (-10% volume decline). In PE compression scenario, HSY faces 58% downside. The 3.2% yield is unsafe if cocoa costs do not normalize.
sell_recommendations.what_would_change_our_mind: Cocoa prices decline 30%+ restoring historical margins; transformative acquisition into non-confectionery categories; GLP-1 adoption plateaus below 20% household penetration

v2_improvements.summary: Comprehensive overhaul addressing all 5 major flaws and 7 missing analyses from judge feedback (score 68/100)

flaw_1_all_buy_recommendations.issue: All 7 picks were BUY/STRONG_BUY at 27.5x sector PE
flaw_1_all_buy_recommendations.fix: Added 3 SELL recommendations (WMT, CL, HSY). Downgraded COST from BUY to HOLD. Portfolio now has 1 STRONG_BUY, 4 BUY, 1 HOLD, 2 SELL, 1 STRONG_SELL -- balanced and conviction-weighted.

flaw_2_cost_scoring_math.issue: COST at 52x PE with 4/10 valuation yet 7.7 overall
flaw_2_cost_scoring_math.fix: Reduced valuation_score to 3/10 and corrected overall_score to 6.8. A stock at 53x PE cannot score above 7 overall regardless of quality. Added explicit scoring_note explaining the correction.

flaw_3_wmt_valuation.issue: WMT at 41x PE (now 45.7x) with 4.9% growth -- where is the justification?
flaw_3_wmt_valuation.fix: Downgraded to SELL. At 45.7x trailing PE (103% above 10yr average) with 4.9% growth and 1% yield, the risk-reward is poor. Even the ad revenue upside case only partially closes the valuation gap. Added detailed sell rationale with price target.

flaw_4_pm_missing_financials.issue: PM missing debt_to_equity and ROE data
flaw_4_pm_missing_financials.fix: Added debt_to_equity of -5.56 and ROE of -132.4% with detailed explanatory notes. Both metrics are negative due to negative shareholder equity from buybacks/acquisitions, NOT operational weakness. Added adjusted ROIC context of 25-30%.

flaw_5_cl_growth_vs_treasuries.issue: CL at 3% growth barely beats Treasuries -- where is the alpha?
flaw_5_cl_growth_vs_treasuries.fix: Downgraded to SELL. Total return math (2.1% yield + 3-4% EPS growth = 5-6%) barely exceeds 4.5% Treasury yield with equity downside risk. Revenue growth actually only 1.4% per latest data -- even worse than previously stated.

new_analyses_added.valuation_heat_map: Current PE vs 5yr avg vs 10yr avg for all picks with color-coded signals (GREEN/YELLOW/RED). Identifies COST, WMT, HSY as DEEP RED and PEP, MDLZ, GIS as GREEN.
new_analyses_added.glp1_impact_quantified: Per-pick GLP-1 revenue impact based on Circana household panel data. PEP most exposed at -1.8% annual drag ($1.7B), HSY at -2.5% (VERY HIGH severity), PM/CL not impacted. Category-level volume changes provided.
new_analyses_added.costco_membership_elasticity: Historical churn data (<1% on 2014 increase), current renewal rates (92.2% US/Canada), fee structure, online signup churn risk, and competitive threats from Sam's Club and Walmart+.
new_analyses_added.pm_iqos_fda_probabilities: Four-scenario analysis with probability-weighted EPS impacts. Full approval at 55% probability (+$0.30-0.50 EPS), conditional at 25%, delay at 15%, denial at 5%.
new_analyses_added.private_label_competition: Category-by-category private label share and threat assessment. Packaged food (26% share, HIGH threat) vs tobacco (2% share, VERY LOW threat). $283B market growing 3x faster than national brands.
new_analyses_added.input_cost_sensitivity: Cocoa (-6%), coffee (-13%), corn (-2%), tobacco leaf (+3%), packaging (+2%) projections with per-pick exposure mapping.
new_analyses_added.walmart_ad_revenue_comparison: WMT Connect $6.4B growing 46% vs Amazon $68B growing 22%. WMT at 1% GMV penetration vs AMZN at 8% shows massive runway. Revenue ratio narrowing from 15:1 to 10.6:1.
new_analyses_added.scenario_analysis: Three scenarios (PE compression 35%, elevated multiples persist 45%, stagflation 20%) with per-pick impact quantification. GIS is only pick with upside in compression scenario.
new_analyses_added.entry_exit_criteria: Position sizing methodology, entry/exit rules, conviction-weighted allocations, and stop-loss triggers.

new_picks_added: GIS (General Mills) -- Deep value contrarian at 10.4x PE, 42% below 10yr average
HSY (Hershey) -- STRONG_SELL, worst risk-reward in sector
